codeReview.summary: Implementation is complete and correct. All four session management methods (create_session, get_session, delete_session, cleanup_expired_sessions) are properly implemented following the existing Database class patterns. The code includes comprehensive docstrings, proper error handling, and automatic timestamp management. All 17 unit tests pass, covering happy paths, edge cases, and integration scenarios. The implementation fulfills assertions m1-a5 and m1-a8 by adding session management methods to the Database abstraction layer.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker successfully followed the backend-worker skill procedure but encountered a Droid-Shield false positive that blocked the git commit. The handoff notes that test placeholder values like 'TEST_API_KEY_1_PLACEHOLDER' in test_database_request_logs.py were flagged as potential secrets. This is a known limitation of secret scanners with test files. The worker completed all implementation and testing work correctly, but couldn't commit due to this blocker.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff whatWasLeftUndone field states: 'Droid-Shield is blocking the git commit due to false positives in test files. The test file tests/unit/test_database_request_logs.py contains test placeholder values like TEST_API_KEY_1_PLACEHOLDER which Droid-Shield incorrectly flags as potential secrets.' Git status shows changes staged but not committed. All 17 tests pass successfully.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The session methods implementation follows the established Database class patterns consistently: automatic timestamp generation for created_at, proper use of insert/fetch_one/delete helper methods, comprehensive docstrings with examples, and returning appropriate types (token string for create, Optional[Row] for get, int for delete/cleanup). This consistency with existing code is good and shows the worker understood the codebase patterns.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Compare create_session() implementation (lines 1302-1345 in database.py) with create_account() (lines 372-425) and create_api_key() (lines 565-600). All follow the same pattern: auto-generate created_at timestamp, build data dict, call self.insert(), return identifier. The cleanup_expired_sessions() method correctly uses datetime comparison with ISO format strings, matching the pattern used throughout the codebase.

summary: Feature m1-f8-session-methods passes code review. All four session management methods are correctly implemented with comprehensive test coverage (17 tests, all passing). The implementation follows existing Database class patterns and conventions. The code is production-ready and fulfills the feature requirements. Note: The commit was blocked by Droid-Shield false positives in unrelated test files, but this is a tooling issue, not a code quality issue. The implementation itself is complete and correct.
